steps.title: Read the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis ($x=0$). The graph crosses the y-axis at $y=9$, giving $(0,9)$.

steps.title: Confirm the form
steps.content:
A y-intercept must have $x=0$, which rules out $(-9,0)$ and $(-6,0)$. Of the points on the y-axis, only $(0,9)$ matches the crossing.

That confirms D is correct.
